Property.title: Property
Property.description: Represents a rental property managed by the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

name.description: A descriptive name or reference for the property (e.g., 'Edificio Centro', 'Casa Frentista').

address.description: The full street address of the property.

unitNumber.description: The specific unit or apartment number within the property (e.g., 'PB A', 'Dpto 3'). Can be '-' for houses or single units.

ownerName.description: The full name of the property owner.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was last updated.

Tenant.title: Tenant
Tenant.description: Represents a tenant (lessee) in the rental system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenant entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the tenant.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the tenant.

email.description: The primary email address for the tenant.

creditScore.description: An optional numerical credit score for the tenant, derived from financial assessments.

openBankingConsentId.description: An identifier linking to the tenant's consent for Open Banking data access, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was last updated.

Contract.title: Contract
Contract.description: Details of a rental contract linking a tenant to a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Contract entity.

tenantId.description: Reference to the Tenant associated with this contract. (Relationship: Tenant 1:N Contract)

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property associated with this contract. (Relationship: Property 1:N Contract)

startDate.description: The official start date of the contract.

endDate.description: The official end date of the contract. (Maps to 'vencimiento' in UI)

baseRentAmount.description: The initial rent amount specified in the contract.

currency.description: The currency in which the rent is primarily denominated (e.g., 'ARS', 'USD').

paymentFrequency.description: How often the rent payments are due (e.g., 'Monthly', 'Quarterly').

adjustmentMechanism.description: The mechanism used for rent adjustments (e.g., 'None', 'ICL', 'IPC', 'CasaPropia', 'Custom').

lastAdjustmentDate.description: The date when the last rent adjustment was applied.

nextAdjustmentDate.description: The date when the next rent adjustment is scheduled.

currentRentAmount.description: The current rent amount, including all applicable adjustments.

administrationFeePercentage.description: The percentage charged as an administration fee on the rent collected (e.g., 0.10 for 10%).

securityDepositAmount.description: The amount of the security deposit held for this contract.

suretyInsuranceProvider.description: The name of the surety insurance provider, if applicable.

suretyInsurancePolicyNumber.description: The policy number for the digital surety insurance, if applicable.

contractFileUrl.description: URL to the scanned or digital copy of the contract document. (Maps to 'tieneContrato' in UI)

status.description: The current status of the contract (e.g., 'Active', 'Overdue', 'InLegal', 'Expired', 'Terminated'). (Maps to 'estado' in UI)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the contract record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the contract record was last updated.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Records a utility bill, communal expense, or other service-related invoice.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

concept.description: A brief description of the invoice's concept (e.g., 'Luz (Edenor) - Espacios Comunes').

amount.description: The total amount of the invoice.

currency.description: The currency of the invoice amount (e.g., 'ARS', 'USD').

issueDate.description: The date the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date the invoice is due. (Maps to 'vencimiento' in UI)

paymentDate.description: The date the invoice was paid.

status.description: The payment status of the invoice (e.g., 'Pending', 'Paid', 'Overdue', 'Cancelled'). (Maps to 'estado' in UI)

receiptFileUrl.description: URL to the uploaded receipt or invoice document. (Maps to 'tieneArchivo' in UI)

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property this invoice is associated with. Optional for general admin invoices. (Relationship: Property 0:N Invoice)

fiscalType.description: The fiscal type of the invoice (e.g., 'Factura B', 'Factura C') for ARCA/AFIP compliance.

fiscalIdentifier.description: The unique fiscal identifier for the electronic invoice (e.g., AFIP CAE).

ocrProcessed.description: Indicates if the invoice was processed via OCR for automated data extraction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was last updated.

MaintenanceRecord.title: MaintenanceRecord
MaintenanceRecord.description: Tracks maintenance tasks and expenses related to properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceRecord entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property where the maintenance was performed. (Relationship: Property 1:N MaintenanceRecord)

concept.description: A description of the maintenance task (e.g., 'Plomería - Reparación caño PB').

costAmount.description: The cost incurred for the maintenance task.

currency.description: The currency of the maintenance cost (e.g., 'ARS', 'USD').

scheduledDate.description: The date when the maintenance was scheduled or performed. (Maps to 'vencimiento' in UI form, implying a date target)

completionDate.description: The date when the maintenance task was completed.

status.description: The current status of the maintenance task (e.g., 'Pending', 'InProgress', 'Completed', 'Cancelled'). (Maps to 'estado' in UI)

documentationFileUrl.description: URL to any relevant documentation (e.g., invoices, photos). (Maps to 'tieneArchivo' in UI)

contractorName.description: The name of the contractor or person who performed the maintenance.

notes.description: Additional notes or details about the maintenance.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance record was last updated.

LegalCase.title: LegalCase
LegalCase.description: Manages records of legal cases or mediation processes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LegalCase entity.

caseType.description: The type of legal proceeding (e.g., 'Eviction', 'Mediation', 'Damages Claim').

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property related to this legal case. Optional if tenant-specific. (Relationship: Property 0:N LegalCase)

tenantId.description: Reference to the Tenant involved in this legal case. Optional if property-specific. (Relationship: Tenant 0:N LegalCase)

contractId.description: Reference to the Contract related to this legal case, if applicable. (Relationship: Contract 0:N LegalCase)

startDate.description: The date when the legal case or mediation process began. (Maps to 'fecha' in UI)

resolutionDate.description: The date when the legal case was resolved.

attorneyName.description: The name of the attorney handling the case. (Maps to 'abogado' in UI)

status.description: The current status of the legal case (e.g., 'Pendiente', 'En proceso', 'Acuerdo firmado', 'Finalizado', 'Closed', 'Cancelled'). (Maps to 'estado' in UI)

caseFileUrl.description: URL to the uploaded legal documents or files. (Maps to 'tieneArchivo' in UI)

description.description: A detailed description of the legal case.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the legal case record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the legal case record was last updated.

Liquidation.title: Liquidation
Liquidation.description: Details of the financial settlement or liquidation report for a property owner.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Liquidation entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property for which this liquidation is generated. (Relationship: Property 1:N Liquidation)

periodStartDate.description: The start date of the period covered by this liquidation.

periodEndDate.description: The end date of the period covered by this liquidation.

totalRentCollectedAmount.description: The total amount of rent collected for the period. (Maps to 'valorAlquiler' in UI)

administrationFeeAmount.description: The amount deducted as administration fees for the period. (Maps to 'valorAdministracion' in UI)

totalMaintenanceDeductions.description: The total amount deducted for maintenance expenses during the period.

totalInvoiceDeductions.description: The total amount deducted for other invoices (e.g., utilities) during the period.

ownerPayoutAmount.description: The final amount to be paid out to the property owner after all deductions. (Maps to 'saldo' in UI)

payoutDate.description: The date when the payout to the owner was made.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the liquidation record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the liquidation record was last updated.

definition.description: Collection for individual rental properties managed by a specific user within an application instance. Each property document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field for authorization independence, linking it directly to the managing user.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application instance, as used in the client-side code (e.g., 'default-app-id').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns and manages these properties.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific property document.

definition.description: Collection for tenant records associated with a specific user and application instance. Each tenant document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence, linking it directly to the managing user.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application instance, as used in the client-side code.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns and manages these tenants.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific tenant document.

definition.description: Collection for rental contracts, linking tenants to properties, specific to a user and application instance. Each contract document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence, linking it directly to the managing user.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application instance, as used in the client-side code.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns and manages these contracts.

params.name: contractId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific contract document.

definition.description: Collection for utility bills, communal expenses, and other service invoices specific to a user and application instance. Each invoice document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence, linking it directly to the managing user.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application instance, as used in the client-side code.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns and manages these invoices.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific invoice document.

definition.description: Collection for tracking maintenance tasks and expenses related to properties, specific to a user and application instance. Each maintenance record document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence, linking it directly to the managing user.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application instance, as used in the client-side code.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns and manages these maintenance records.

params.name: maintenanceRecordId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific maintenance record document.

definition.description: Collection for managing legal cases and mediations related to properties or tenants, specific to a user and application instance. Each legal case document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence, linking it directly to the managing user.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application instance, as used in the client-side code.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns and manages these legal cases.

params.name: legalCaseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific legal case document.

definition.description: Collection for financial settlement reports for property owners, specific to a user and application instance. Each liquidation document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence, linking it directly to the managing user.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application instance, as used in the client-side code.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns and manages these liquidations.

params.name: liquidationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific liquidation document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to leverage the existing application's user-centric data model while significantly enhancing security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The current React client already organizes data under a user-specific path (`artifacts/{appId}/users/{user.uid}/{collectionName}`), which forms a strong foundation for private, user-owned data.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design prioritizes authorization independence by mandating the denormalization of the `ownerId` (which will be `request.auth.uid`) into *every single document* at all levels of the hierarchy. For example, a `Property` document at `/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/properties/{propertyId}` will not only be under the `userId` path segment but will also contain a field `ownerId: {userId}`. This makes security rules extremely simple, atomic, and efficient: `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId;`. This eliminates any reliance on `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document ownership, which would otherwise introduce complexity, break atomic operations (like transactions or batch writes), and make debugging difficult.

**QAPs (Queries as Permissions):**
This structure inherently supports the Queries as Permissions principle through **Structural Segregation**. All data managed by a user is segregated under their unique `userId` path. This means that a query to `/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/properties` will *only* return properties owned by `userId`. Because the security rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid` must match `userId` in the path (and `ownerId` in the document data), any `list` operation performed by an authenticated user will automatically be constrained to their own data. There is no need for complex `where` clauses in security rules or client-side filtering after unauthorized data has been fetched, making queries secure by default and simplifying client-side logic.
